title: delete
text: delete Deletes the current occurrence from the database. delete Return Values Table 1. Values returned in $status Value Meaning 0 Data was successfully deleted. -1 No entities are painted on the component. -2 Occurrence not found. -3 Exceptional I/O error (hardware or software). -5 Update request for non-updatable occurrence. -6 Exceptional I/O error on write request; for example, lack of disk space, no write permission, or violation of a database constraint. Check the message frame for details. -11 Occurrence already locked. -16 Network error: unknown. Table 2. Values commonly returned by $procerror following delete Value Error constant Meaning -2 through -12 <UIOSERR_*> Errors during database I/O. -16 through -30 <UNETERR_*> Errors during network I/O. -1 <UGENERR_ERROR> An error occurred. No entities are painted on the component. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The delete statement deletes the current occurrence from the database. CAUTION: use this statement only in the delete trigger and, with extreme caution, in the deleteUp trigger. If you do place it in another trigger, make sure that you lock the occurrence as soon as possible. Example: Using delete trigger Delete delete end ; Delete Copy Related Links erase Activate the delete or deleteUp trigger for all occurrences in the component. remocc Mark an occurrence of the specified entity for deletion at the next store. store Initiate validation and storage of all occurrences marked as modified. It activates the appropriate validation triggers as required, followed by the write and delete triggers. trigger delete Removes occurrences marked for deletion, when writing updates to the database. trigger deleteUp System trigger that handles deletion of Up entity occurrences. (An Up entity is an inner entity that represents the One side in a one-to-many relationship.) trigger store Trigger that responds to a request to save (store) the data. Was this topic helpful?
